title: P1-03: CSP strictness parser + HSTS parameter validation
acceptance_criteria: --- CSP STRICTNESS (new bespoke function, NOT a table entry) ---
Add a checkCsp(crawl: CrawlResult): RawFinding[] function modelled on the existing checkSri() pattern — it constructs its own findings, does NOT extend the HEADER_CHECKS table.

Parse the 'content-security-policy' header value from crawl.headers (keys are lower-cased). If absent, return [] (the existing HEADER_CHECKS entry already emits the MEDIUM absent-CSP finding; do not double-emit).
Parse 'content-security-policy-report-only' separately from the same crawl.headers map. A weak report-only CSP emits severity=INFO (not HIGH), with title noting it is report-only and not enforced.
Determine the effective script policy: prefer the 'script-src' directive; fall back to 'default-src' if 'script-src' is absent.

Flag as HIGH (title: 'Weak Content-Security-Policy: unsafe-inline allowed') when 'unsafe-inline' appears as a standalone keyword in the effective directive, UNLESS a nonce ('nonce-...') or hash ('sha256-...', 'sha384-...', 'sha512-...') is also present in the same directive — in CSP2+ these neutralise unsafe-inline.
Flag as HIGH (title: 'Weak Content-Security-Policy: unsafe-eval allowed') when 'unsafe-eval' appears as a standalone keyword in the effective directive.

Flag as HIGH (title: 'Overly permissive Content-Security-Policy: wildcard source') when a bare * or bare http: or bare https: appears as a source expression in the effective directive. Do NOT flag scoped wildcards like *.googleapis.com.
Do NOT flag any directive as weak when 'strict-dynamic' is also present — strict-dynamic neutralises host-source allowlists and unsafe-inline in CSP3, making the policy effectively nonce/hash-only.
Call checkCsp() from runHeadersModule() and push its results into the findings array (same call site as checkSri()).
--- HSTS VALIDATION (new bespoke function) ---
Add a checkHsts(crawl: CrawlResult): RawFinding[] function.
If the 'strict-transport-security' header is absent, return [] (the existing HEADER_CHECKS entry already emits MEDIUM; do not double-emit).
Extract max-age using a regex. If max-age < 15_768_000 (≈6 months), emit severity=LOW, title='Strict-Transport-Security max-age is too short', evidence shows the actual value.
If max-age is present and >= 15_768_000 but the directive does NOT include 'includesubdomains' (case-insensitive), emit severity=INFO, title='Strict-Transport-Security missing includeSubDomains'.
If HSTS is well-formed (max-age >= 15_768_000, includeSubDomains present), emit nothing.
Call checkHsts() from runHeadersModule() and push its results.
--- SEVERITY RECALIBRATION ---
No severity recalibrations in this task (X-Frame-Options stays LOW, SRI stays LOW — per Gate 1 decision).
--- TESTS ---

Add tests for: unsafe-inline (HIGH), unsafe-eval (HIGH), bare wildcard (HIGH), nonce+unsafe-inline (no finding), hash+unsafe-inline (no finding), strict-dynamic+unsafe-inline (no finding), scoped wildcard (no finding), report-only weak CSP (INFO), absent CSP (no new finding from checkCsp), HSTS short max-age (LOW), HSTS good max-age missing includeSubDomains (INFO), HSTS fully correct (no finding).
All existing P1-03 tests still pass.
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
